description_long: This sculpture is a robust, classical-style torso of a male figure, rendered without arms, head, or lower legs. The anatomical details are highly pronounced, with muscular contours and a dynamic, twisting posture that suggests movement or tension. The surface is textured and uneven, giving the impression of aged stone or bronze with weathered patina. The base is a simple, rectangular plinth that appears to be made of a different material, possibly stone or concrete, providing stability and contrast to the organic form above. The coloration is predominantly grayish with hints of green and brown, likely simulating oxidation or natural aging. The overall impression is one of strength and timelessness, reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman statuary.
description: A weathered, muscular male torso sculpture with no head, arms, or lower legs, mounted on a rectangular plinth. The surface is textured and aged, evoking classical antiquity.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed chest, abdomen, and upper thighs, with pronounced musculature and a dynamic twist.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the curvature of the torso and the depth of the sculpted muscles, with the left hip and shoulder more visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broad back, defined shoulder blades, and the rounded form of the lower back and buttocks.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the right flank, with visible ribcage and the twist of the torso.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a plinth, suggesting it is intended for display in a public or semi-public space such as a park, plaza, or gallery.
